column1:
Insurance bets are offered whenever the dealer shows an ace. They are half your bet, and pay 2 to 1 odds.

You may split a pair or surrender whenever you have only two cards.

You may double down if your cards total 10 or 11 points.
column2:
莊家出 A 就可買保險，賭金是下注的一半，贏率 2:1。

任何時刻只有兩張牌可以分牌或投降。

牌數合計 10 或 11 點可加倍下注。

column1:
Press F4 when ready to play.

Press F6 for another card.

Press F8 to stand.

To change the amount of your bet, type "change bet."
column2:
準備好按 F4 開始。

要牌按 F6。

停牌按 F8。

改下注金額輸入「change bet」。

column1: You are in the Casino's hotel. Doors line each side, many with "Do Not Disturb" signs on their doorknobs.
column2: 你在賭場旅館內。四周盡是旅館房門，許多門把上掛著「請勿打擾」的牌子。

column1: There's a door with a heart on it to the west.
column2: 西邊有扇心形門。

column1: Not one for those long elevator rides, eh, Larry?
column2: 不喜歡長坐電梯啊，Larry？

column1: You rap your knuckles on the door. There is no response, but now your knuckles hurt.
column2: 你敲門。沒人應，反而敲痛了自己的手。

column1: Fawn's delicate voice booms out across the hotel:\n\n"Just a minute, Larry!"
column2: Fawn 嬌柔的聲音在旅館內炸開：\n\n「稍等，Larry！」

column1: There's a click as she unlocks the door, then a piercing "Come on in. The door's open."
column2: 咔一聲開鎖，接著傳來尖銳的「進來，門開了。」

column1: You can't. This door is locked.
column2: 不行。門鎖了。

column1: Subtly, you shout, "Here I come, baby!"
column2: 你喊道：「寶貝，我來了！」（一點也不含蓄）

column1: It's a door not unlike all the others, except this one has a cute, trite, cracked, little, plastic heart stuck to it with a thumbtack.
column2: 跟其他門沒兩樣，除了用圖釘黏著一個可愛又俗氣、裂開的小塑膠心。

column1: It's a door just like all the others.
column2: 就是普通的旅館房門。

column1: They almost look real, don't they?
column2: 看起來挺真實的，是吧？

column1: You seem surprised that the door is locked.
column2: 你看起來驚訝門被鎖了。

column1: You close the door behind you, and jiggle the knob to confirm it is locked.
column2: 你關上身後的門，扭了一下把手確認門鎖著。

column1: Isn't it funny how the elevator is always waiting for you?
column2: 電梯老是為你等著，挺諷刺吧？

column1: The cheap, plastic heart is not worth the bother.
column2: 廉價塑膠心不值得費力。

column1: Fawn's sexy voice oozes through the door, "Oh, baby, it's you! You're back!!"
column2: Fawn 性感的聲音從門裡滲出來：「喔寶貝，是你！你回來了！！」

column1: There's a click as she unlocks the door. "Come on in, you wonderful hunk!"
column2: 咔一聲開鎖。「進來，你這性感的傢伙！」

column1: It's no good. The door resists all of your efforts.
column2: 沒轍。門硬是不動。

column1: You contemplate expec- toration, but remember that discretion is the better part of valium.
column2: 你想吐，但想起沉著才是冷靜之母。

column1: ...or helium,
column2: …或是氦氣之類的。

column1: ...or something.
column2: …某個什麼啦。

column1: Suicide is illegal here.
column2: 這裡自殺犯法。

column1: You rap your knuckles on the nearest door.
column2: 你敲了最近的那扇門。

column1: This must not be the right door, Larry.
column2: Larry，這肯定不是對的門。

column1: Hey, Scott, the girls are here!!
column2: 「嘿，Scott，女孩們來了！！」

column1: It says 'Do NOT Disturb!' Can't you read?
column2: 「上面寫『請勿打擾』！你不識字？」

column1: You hear some extremely heavy breathing.
column2: 你聽到極度粗重的呼吸聲。

column1: Go away! I gave at the office!
column2: 「滾開！我在辦公室已經獻了！」

column1: Oh, Russ, there's someone at the door!\n\n"They're gonna have to wait, Kim Baby! It's too late for me to stop now!!"
column2: 「喔，Russ，有人敲門！」\n\n「他們得等著，Kim 寶貝！我現在停不下來了！！」

column1: Baaaaah. Baaaaah!\n\nDon't even ask, Larry!
column2: 「巴—巴—」\n\nLarry，別問，真的別問。

column1:
Oh, Bruce!
Oh, Howard!

Oh, Terri!
Oh, Carrie!
column2:
「喔，Bruce！
喔，Howard！

喔，Terri！
喔，Carrie！」

column1: Ken! Get off me, Ken!!
column2: 「Ken！下來，Ken！！」

column1: Oh, gawd, Ed!! Get in the closet. It's my husband!!
column2: 「天哪，Ed！！躲衣櫥裡。是我老公！！」

column1: Bobbit, is that you?
column2: 「Bobbit，是你嗎？」

column1: Not now; I've got wessonality!
column2: 「現在不行，我脂肪慾旺！」

column1: Is that Room Service? I didn't order anchovies.
column2: 「客房服務？我沒點鯷魚啊。」

column1: An eye appears at the peephole. "Hey, Mary! Isn't it a little early for Trick or Treaters?"
column2: 從貓眼裡出現一隻眼睛。「嘿，Mary！要糖果日還早吧？」

column1: Oh, gawd, Ed!! Get in the closet. It's my wife!!
column2: 「天哪，Ed！！躲衣櫥裡。是我老婆！！」

column1: Hmmm. Is that the smell of burning rubber?
column2: 嗯？那是輪胎燒焦的味道？

column1: Oh, Jeff. You really know how to hurt a guy!
column2: 「喔，Jeff。你真會傷人！」

column1: Get away from him, you big bully! He's mine.
column2: 「滾開，你這大壞蛋！他是我的。」

column1: Your are in the casino hotel's honeymoon suite. The heart-shaped bed is just waiting for you and Fawn. The dim lighting provides the perfect atmosphere. You've always appreciated the "Early American Gaudy" look. There is a radio on the shelf behind the bed.
column2: 你在賭場旅館的蜜月套房。心形床靜靜等著你和 Fawn。昏黃燈光營造完美氛圍。你向來欣賞那種「美式奢華」風格。床後的架子上放著一台收音機。

column1: This is the honeymoon suite. The heart-shaped bed would have been perfect for you and Fawn. Too bad things worked out like this!
column2: 這是蜜月套房。心形床本來是你和 Fawn 的完美去處。可惜事與願違！

column1: The radio interrupts the Frank Sinatra medley with an obnoxious singing commercial...
column2: 收音機打斷 Frank Sinatra 的歌曲組合，放起討人厭的歌唱廣告……

column1:
We're Ajax Liquor,
and we're proud to say,
we deliver all across
the U.S.A.
column2:
「我們是 Ajax 酒類，
我們驕傲地說，
我們送遍
整個美國。」

column1:
You're out of luck
if you want to dine,
but if you just need booze,
dial 555-8039!
column2: 「想吃飯沒運氣，\n但要喝酒有辦法，\n打電話 555-8039！」

column1: Sounds like a nice mellow station...
column2: 聽起來是不錯的輕音樂頻道……

column1: And now, back to our music.
column2: 「現在回到我們的音樂。」

column1: Click.
column2: 喀。

column1: She doesn't appear to be in the mood yet, Larry.
column2: Larry，她看起來還沒做好準備。

column1: Good idea. Unfortunately, you have nothing with which to cut a rope, Larry.
column2: 好主意。可惜 Larry，你沒有剪繩子的東西。

column1: Right!\n\nUsing the pocket knife you got from the old bum by the convenience store, you saw through the ropes holding you to the bed.
column2: 對了！\n\n你用從便利店外那個乞丐手上拿到的小刀，把綁著你的繩子鋸斷。

column1: You never know when a nice piece of rope will come in handy.
column2: 一條好繩子遲早有用。

column1: Since you have no way of cutting the ropes, and they're far too tightly tied for you to loosen, it looks like this is the end for you, Larry.
column2: 由於無法剪繩子，繩子又綁得死緊，Larry，看來你完蛋了。

column1: You can't. She fastened them quite securely!
column2: 不行。她綁得牢牢的！

column1: You can't. You're tied to a bed!
column2: 不行。你被綁在床上！

column1: She is so beautiful you want to do wonderful things to, er, ah, FOR her.
column2: 她太漂亮了，你想為她…不對，跟她…做美好的事。

column1: You know, a little wine would help me get in the mood, Larry.
column2: 「你知道，點紅酒會讓我比較有心情，Larry。」

column1: I'd think the least a gentleman would do is to buy a lady a bottle of wine.
column2: 「紳士最起碼應該買瓶酒給女士吧。」

column1: She looks ready, Larry.
column2: Larry，她看起來準備好了。

column1: She says, "Lie down, Larry. I have a special surprise for you!"
column2: 她說：「躺下，Larry。我有個驚喜給你！」

column1: Then she says, "Close your eyes," and you do because you're sure that, finally, success is at hand.
column2: 接著她說：「閉上眼睛。」你依言照做，因為你確信終於要成功了。

column1: Oh, Fawn! you moan.
column2: 「喔，Fawn！」你呻吟。

column1: You grab your wallet and look inside.\n\nOh, well. At least she didn't find the %v90 bucks you hid in that secret compartment. With your consummate gambling skills, you should be back on your feet in no time!
column2: 你掏出錢包看裡面。\n\n算了。至少她沒發現你藏在暗格裡的 %v90 塊。以你高超的賭技，馬上就能東山再起！

column1: You're tied to the bed and can't move.
column2: 你被綁在床上，動不了。

column1: When Fawn has you securely tied to the bed, she grabs your wallet, saying, "You forgot to pay me for the wine," then heads out the door.
column2: 當 Fawn 把你綁牢後，她搶了你的錢包，說：「你忘了付我酒錢。」然後走出門。

column1: FAWN! Come back! you shout. "This isn't funny!!"
column2: 「Fawn！回來！」你喊。「這一點也不好笑！！」

column1: Aren't you going to pour the wine? she says, her eyes flashing.
column2: 「你不倒酒？」她說，眼神閃閃發光。

column1: I'm ready when you are, honey!\n\nCome on, Larry; what are you waiting for?
column2: 「你準備好我就準備，親愛的！」\n\nLarry，你在等什麼？

column1: She looks like she could use some of that wine.
column2: 她看起來需要點那瓶酒。

column1: There's no wine here.
column2: 這裡沒酒。

column1: Right. Sure. Pour another for her while you're at it!
column2: 對啊，倒一杯給她吧！

column1: You grab the bottle, and fortify your courage with a long, hard belt!
column2: 你抓起酒瓶，喝了長長一口給自己壯膽！

column1: You carefully remove the bottle from the wine bucket, unscrew the cap, and pour two glasses. She downs her glass immediately, you refill it, and she does it again. Her eyes meet yours, and you're sure luck is with you this time!
column2: 你小心地從酒桶裡取出酒瓶，擰開蓋子，倒了兩杯。她一口乾了，你又倒，她再乾一杯。眼神相交，你確信這次交好運了！

column1: This room doesn't even have a telephone!
column2: 這房間根本沒電話！

column1: It's pretty complicated. There's a knob that says "On."
column2: 挺複雜的。有個寫著「開」的旋鈕。

column1: The radio IS off.
column2: 收音機確實關了。

column1: The radio IS on.
column2: 收音機確實開著。

column1:
Ooh, Baby!

That feels so good!

Ooooooooooh!!
column2:
「喔，寶貝！

好舒服！

喔—！」

column1: Hey, what's the deal with the rope?
column2: 「嘿，為什麼綁繩子？」

column1: Why are you tying me to the bed?
column2: 「你為什麼要把我綁在床上？」

column1: You have more important things on your mind than sitting.
column2: 你腦子裡想的比坐著重要得多。

column1: This window doesn't open.
column2: 窗戶打不開。

column1: The tuning knob on the radio is stuck. Looks like you're stuck with the Easy Listening station.
column2: 收音機的調頻鈕卡住了。看來你得聽輕音樂頻道了。

column1: You can't move the knob, but it doesn't matter since the radio isn't on anyway!
column2: 旋鈕轉不動，不過反正收音機沒開，也就無所謂了。

column1: You see the lights of Lost Wages spread out before you.
column2: 眼前鋪開 Lost Wages 的夜景。

column1: Oh, Larry, it's you! Fawn says. "I thought it was that cute, young delivery boy again."
column2: 「喔，Larry，是你！」Fawn 說。「我以為又是那個可愛的年輕外送員。」

column1: Hmmm. Was "Tuesday" a good year?
column2: 嗯？「星期二」是好年份嗎？

column1: It's a table model, not a portable.
column2: 是桌上型的，不是便攜式。

column1: Besides, the hotel bolted it to the table.
column2: 況且旅館把它鎖在桌子上。

column1: You are unable to break the window.
column2: 你破不了窗戶。

column1: You can't. It's the same brand that people can't break when they want to christen a ship!
column2: 不行。這牌子的玻璃連人家要砸碎它來命名船隻時都砸不碎！

column1: Don't you think a heart-shaped bed is trite?
column2: 你不覺得心形床俗氣嗎？

column1: You can't. The delivery boy glued it to the table.
column2: 不行。外送員把它膠死在桌子上。

column1: FAWN!! You scum-sucking gutter slug, get back in here!
column2: 「Fawn！！你這下水道裡的爛蛞蝓，滾回來！」

column1: Unfortunately, your pleas go unheeded.
column2: 可惜沒人聽你的哀求。

column1: Say, Fawn, when are you going to get undressed?
column2: 「嘿，Fawn，你什麼時候脫衣服？」

column1: Hmmm. You wonder if there's any way a guy can "consummate" by himself?
column2: 嗯？你在想有沒有辦法一個人「圓滿」？

column1: Larry, it looks like "the honeymoon's over."
column2: Larry，看來「蜜月結束了。」

column1: As she heads out the door, she laughs and says, "So long, turkey!"
column2: 她邊走邊笑著說：「拜了，蠢蛋！」

column1: %m14 A beautiful security guard sits behind a desk.
column2: %m14 一位漂亮保安坐在辦公桌後。

column1: She takes one look at the bottle of Spanish Fly and says, "Boy, thanks! How did you know that I love this stuff?!" She tosses down a couple of pills, then a few more, and finally gulps the whole bottle! Her breathing becomes faster and faster until she begins to pant.
column2: 她看到西班牙蒼蠅藥瓶眼睛一亮，說：「天哪，謝謝！你怎麼知道我喜歡這玩意？！」她吞了幾粒，再吞幾粒，最後整瓶灌下去！呼吸越來越急促，直到開始喘息。

column1: She is a knockout! But that pair of pistols slung around her hips brings a sense of foreboding to your gut.
column2: 她漂亮死了！但腰間那對槍帶給你一股不祥的預感。

column1: (Looks like tonight's your lucky night, Larry.)
column2: （看來今晚是你的幸運日，Larry。）

column1: The rumors were right. You are quick on the trigger, Larry!
column2: 謠言沒錯。你這傢伙確實很急色，Larry！

column1: Try it, and I'll blow your head off.
column2: 「敢動歪念，我就開槍斃了你。」

column1: Evidently, this exit is for employees only. You'd better use the elevator.
column2: 很明顯，這出口只限員工。還是用電梯吧。

column1: The beautiful security guard is sitting at the desk. A pair of large pistols are in a holster around her slim waist. She looks friendly, but quick on the trigger.
column2: 漂亮的保安坐在辦公桌後。她纖細腰間的全套槍套亮閃閃的。看起來友善，但扳機很快。

column1: Since you're no longer staring at Faith's ample weapons, you notice an unlabeled button on her desk.
column2: 既然你不再盯著 Faith 的「宏偉武器」看，你注意到她辦公桌上有個沒標籤的按鈕。

column1: Oh, oh. Where are we goin' now, Larry?
column2: 咦呀，現在去哪，Larry？

column1: You are at the top floor of the hotel.
column2: 你在旅館頂樓。

column1: %m14 That desk is where you lost your Faith, remember? (So glad there was a chance to use THAT line!)
column2: %m14 就是在那張辦公桌你失去了 Faith，記得吧？（終於用上這句話了！）

column1: A brass plate near the elevator says: "Private."
column2: 電梯旁的銅牌寫著：「私人專用」。

column1: Hi, my name's Faith. Nice suit!
column2: 「嗨，我叫 Faith。不錯的西裝！」

column1: Bad breath's one thing, Mister; but, you could knock a buzzard off a manure truck!
column2: 「口臭算是小事，仁兄，但你能把禿鷲從糞堆上打下來！」

column1: Touch that button, and you're dead meat, White-Suit!
column2: 「敢碰那個按鈕，白西裝，你死定了！」

column1: So long! Faith says. "I've got to find my boy friend before this stuff wears off!"
column2: 「拜了！」Faith 說。「得在藥效退前找到我男友！」

column1: The penthouse elevator doors glide open.
column2: 頂樓電梯門滑開。
